basic_details.job_title: DTL Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 63 Posts
basic_details.application_mode: Offline (Application to be sent after NATS Registration)
basic_details.short_information: Delhi Transco Limited (DTL), a Govt. of NCT of Delhi Undertaking and a State Transmission Utility operating at EHV level (220kV and above), has issued a notification for engagement of Graduate and Technician Apprentices for FY 2026-27 under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The engagement is being carried out under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS), administered by the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BoAT), Northern Region. The notification number is F/DTL/HR(Trg.)Apprenticeship/2026-27/F-14/83, dated 16 July 2026. The apprenticeship training period is one year.

age_limit.age_details: Minimum: 18 Years (No upper age limit mentioned)
age_limit.details: The notification specifies a minimum age of 18 years at the time of application. No upper age limit is mentioned in the official notification.

qualification.education_qualification: B.E./B.Tech (Electrical/EEE/IT/CSE/Civil), Diploma in Electrical Engineering, B.A./B.Sc./BBA etc.
qualification.details: The candidate must be an Indian citizen and 18 years old or above at the time of application. The candidate must have passed the requisite full-time Degree/Diploma in the respective cadre from a recognized University/Board in India within five (05) years of the closing date of application, and must be medically fit as per the provisions of the Apprentices Act, 1961. Candidates who have undergone, are undergoing, or are already registered in a NATS contract for training under the Apprenticeship Act elsewhere are not eligible. Candidates with work experience of one or more years are not eligible. | apprentice category: Qualification | graduate apprentice (engineering): B.E./B.Tech in Electrical/EEE, IT/CSE, or Civil Engineering | technician apprentice: Diploma in Electrical Engineering | graduate apprentice (non-engineering): B.A./B.Sc./BBA etc.

salary_details.details: The stipend will be paid for 12 months of Apprenticeship. Of this amount, 50% will be paid by DTL and the remaining 50% by the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT), NR, Govt. of India, through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into the registered bank account of the Apprentice.

selection_process: The notification does not mention any written test
or other selection stage beyond the First Come First Serve basis described above.
how_to_apply: Register as a "STUDENT" on the Apprenticeship portal: www.nats.education.gov.in, and obtain a valid APAR ID.
Download the application form from the DTL website: https://dtl.gov.in
Fill the application form in all respects.
Send the duly filled application form so that it reaches the address given below within the stipulated date and time (31 July 2026, 5:30 PM):
O/o Sr. Manager (T) Training Delhi Transco Limited Training Department (HR) Delhi Transco Limited, Room No. 306, 3rd Floor, Shakti Sadan, Kotla Road, New Delhi - 110002 Contact: 011-23214149
The notification does not specify a separate walk-in date or venue for document verification/selection beyond the application submission address above.

rows.details: B.E./B.Tech (Electrical/EEE/IT/CSE/Civil), Diploma in Electrical Engineering, B.A./B.Sc./BBA etc.
Minimum: 18 Years (No upper age limit mentioned)
Offline (Application to be sent after NATS Registration)

content_sections.heading: DTL Apprentice Vacancy Details 2026-27
content_sections.text: The notification lists tentative training slots across three apprentice streams. DTL has clarified that the number of slots is provisional/tentative and may vary according to the actual requirement of the company and in compliance with the Apprentices Act, 1961, at the time of engagement. Note: The serial numbering (1, 2, 4) and the omission of Sr. No. 3 appear exactly as printed in the official notification. Apprentice Trainees will be engaged for any location/office within NCT of Delhi, as per the requirement of the organisation.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: The candidate must be an Indian citizen and 18 years old or above at the time of application. The candidate must have passed the requisite full-time Degree/Diploma in the respective cadre from a recognized University/Board in India within five (05) years of the closing date of application, and must be medically fit as per the provisions of the Apprentices Act, 1961. Candidates who have undergone, are undergoing, or are already registered in a NATS contract for training under the Apprenticeship Act elsewhere are not eligible. Candidates with work experience of one or more years are not eligible.

rows.qualification: B.E./B.Tech in Electrical/EEE, IT/CSE, or Civil Engineering

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: The notification specifies a minimum age of 18 years at the time of application. No upper age limit is mentioned in the official notification.

content_sections.heading: Stipend (Salary)
content_sections.text: The stipend will be paid for 12 months of Apprenticeship. Of this amount, 50% will be paid by DTL and the remaining 50% by the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT), NR, Govt. of India, through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into the registered bank account of the Apprentice.

content_sections.heading: Leave Entitlement
content_sections.text: Apprentices are entitled to 08 days of Casual Leave and 15 days of Medical Leave (on medical illness grounds), on a pro-rata basis, for one year of Apprenticeship duration.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: The notification does not mention any written test, interview, or other selection stage beyond the First Come First Serve basis described above.

content_sections.heading: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) and Aadhaar Seeding Requirement
content_sections.text: The candidate should have an active Aadhaar-seeded bank account, and the same should be DBT-enabled for Direct Benefit Transfer as per Govt. of India norms. These bank details must be submitted in the online application while applying. The Name and Date of Birth of the applicant should be the same across the Matriculation (10th class) certificate, Aadhaar Card, and bank account. Candidates can check their Bank Seeding status at: https://myaadhar.uidai.gov.in/bank-seeding-status

content_sections.heading: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Candidates must report to the venue with a duly filled application form (available on the DTL website) along with one set of self-attested photocopies and two recent passport-size colour photographs, plus the following original documents: 10th/12th Marksheet Degree/Diploma certificate or Provisional Certificate All semester marksheets (in case marks are in GPA/CGPA, as per University guidelines for converting GPA/CGPA to percentage) Valid PAN Card Valid Aadhaar Card APAR ID Bank account details/Cancelled Cheque etc. (DBT-enabled account details) Caste/PWD Certificate issued by the Competent Authority (for reserved category applicants) Details of registration on the NATS Portal

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for DTL Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27
content_sections.text: Register as a "STUDENT" on the Apprenticeship portal: www.nats.education.gov.in , and obtain a valid APAR ID. Download the application form from the DTL website: https://dtl.gov.in Fill the application form in all respects. Send the duly filled application form so that it reaches the address given below within the stipulated date and time (31 July 2026, 5:30 PM): O/o Sr. Manager (T) Training Delhi Transco Limited Training Department (HR) Delhi Transco Limited, Room No. 306, 3rd Floor, Shakti Sadan, Kotla Road, New Delhi - 110002 Contact: 011-23214149 The notification does not specify a separate walk-in date or venue for document verification/selection beyond the application submission address above.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for DTL Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for receiving applications is 31 July 2026, up to 5:30 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in DTL Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are tentatively 25 slots for Graduate Apprentice (Engineering) (Electrical/EEE – 21, IT/CSE – 02, Civil – 02), 25 slots for Technician Apprentice (Diploma in Electrical Engineering), and 13 slots for Graduate Apprentices (Non-Engineering). DTL has stated these numbers are provisional and may change based on actual requirement.

faq.question: Q3. What is the stipend for DTL apprentices?
faq.answer: Degree holders receive Rs. 12,300 per month and Diploma holders receive Rs. 10,900 per month, paid for 12 months, with 50% contributed by DTL and 50% by the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT), NR, through DBT.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for DTL Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants must be Indian citizens, at least 18 years old, and must have passed the requisite full-time Degree/Diploma from a recognized University/Board in India within the last five years of the closing date of application. Candidates already registered in a NATS contract elsewhere, or with one or more years of work experience, are not eligible.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee for DTL Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: Not mentioned in the official notification.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for DTL Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is on a First Come First Serve basis, based on eligibility criteria, the prescribed qualification for the respective trade, and the number of training slots notified. No written exam or interview is mentioned in the notification.
